devanagari: प्रावृड्वर्णनं शरद्वर्णनं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । तयोस्तदद्‍भुतं कर्म दावाग्नेर्मोक्षमात्मनः । गोपाः स्त्रीभ्यः समाचख्युः प्रलम्बवधमेव च ॥ १...
english_devnagari: prāvṛḍvarṇanaṃ śaradvarṇanaṃ ca śrīśuka uvāca tayostadad‍bhutaṃ karma dāvāgnermokṣamātmanaḥ gopāḥ strībhyaḥ samācakhyuḥ pralambavadhameva ca

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said*:
The cowherd-boys related to their mothers and other women-folk (particularizing the special points) in details, the miraculous feats of them both (viz. of Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) as to how Kṛṣṇa rescued them from a forest-conflagration, and how Balarāma killed the demon Pralamba.

devanagari: गोपवृद्धाश्च गोप्यश्च तदुपाकर्ण्य विस्मिताः । मेनिरे देवप्रवरौ कृष्णरामौ व्रजं गतौ ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: gopavṛddhāśca gopyaśca tadupākarṇya vismitāḥ menire devapravarau kṛṣṇarāmau vrajaṃ gatau

[nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]

translation: Listening to the account, elderly cowherds and cowherd women were astonished. They (thenceforth) looked upon Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma as the most prominent gods who have come down to stay in Vraja.

devanagari: ततः प्रावर्तत प्रावृट् सर्वसत्त्वसमुद्‍भवा । विद्योतमानपरिधिः विस्फूर्जित नभस्तला ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ prāvartata prāvṛṭ sarvasattvasamud‍bhavā vidyotamānaparidhiḥ visphūrjita nabhastalā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: [1] Then (after the summer), there was the advent of the rainy season which is favourable to the regeneration of all beings, and during which, the sky appears beautiful with refulgent circles (round the Sun and the Moon), and is resounded with thunders.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Note: VV. 3-24 give a spiritually suggestive, beautiful description of the rainy season in the epic style. Annotators have tried to find some ‘hidden meaning’ convenient to their school of thought in these verses.
Padaratnāvalī regards this description as an illustration (dṛṣṭānta) of the deep or superficial (of pure and not so pure) devotion to god Nārāyaṇa.
(nārāyaṇa-bhakti-vaiśadyāvaiśadya-dṛṣṭāntatvena prāvid ṛtum varṇayati /)

devanagari: सान्द्रनीलाम्बुदैर्व्योम सविद्युत् स्तनयित्‍नुभिः । अस्पष्टज्योतिः आच्छन्नं ब्रह्मेव सगुणं बभौ ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sāndranīlāmbudairvyoma savidyut stanayit‍nubhiḥ aspaṣṭajyotiḥ ācchannaṃ brahmeva saguṇaṃ babhau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Being overspread with thick dark blue clouds (heavy with water), attended with lightning and thunder, and with its light (of the Sun or the Moon) being obscured and dimmed, the sky looked like Brahman modified by the conditioning guṇas of Prakṛti[2] (or the individual soul whose spiritual wisdom is obscured by sattva, rajas and tamas each corresponding to the lightning, thunder and clouds which overcast the sky)[3].
purport:
Footnote [2]:
saguṇa brahman—(1) Individual soul conditioned by guṇas but capable of expanding by guṇas and not physically—Bhāgavata Candrikā (2) Four-faced god Brahmā—Padaratnāvalī

Footnote [3]:
(i) Bhāgavata Candrikā: By nature, the sky is clear, pure and bright but gets darkened by clouds etc. So also though knowledge is inherent in the Jīva, the cognitive power remains ineffective till the Jīva attains Liberation or mukti. Hence one should try for Liberation.
(ii) The Sun: the brightest luminary is shrouded with clouds like god Brahmā with the three guṇas.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: अष्टौ मासान् निपीतं यद् भूम्याश्चोदमयं वसु । स्वगोभिर्मोक्तुमारेभे पर्जन्यः काल आगते ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: aṣṭau māsān nipītaṃ yad bhūmyāścodamayaṃ vasu svagobhirmoktumārebhe parjanyaḥ kāla āgate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ aś class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: (Like a king redistributing his wealth for public welfare to his subjects, from whom he collected it in the form of land revenue and other taxes). At the proper time (in the rainy season), the Sun-god or the Rainy season[4] began to release the wealth in the form of water to the earth from which he absorbed it with his rays, during the (last) eight months.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Parjanya—The sun-god who presides over the thundering clouds.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: तडिद्वन्तो महामेघाः चण्ड श्वसन वेपिताः । प्रीणनं जीवनं ह्यस्य मुमुचुः करुणा इव ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: taḍidvanto mahāmeghāḥ caṇḍa śvasana vepitāḥ prīṇanaṃ jīvanaṃ hyasya mumucuḥ karuṇā iva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vip ], [vocative plural from √ vip ]
[nominative plural from √ vip ], [vocative plural from √ vip ], [accusative plural from √ vip ]

translation: Mighty clouds charged with lightning (with which they viewed the earth parched with the summer heat) and violently tossed by tempestuous winds (as if they were overwhelmed with compassion for the world afflicted with the summer) showered down the life-giving[5] water (resuscitating the world) which brought delight to all, just as merciful persons[6] lay down their lives out of compassion for the harassed and tormented people.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
v.l. prāṇinām—The very life of living beings—Bhāgavata Candrikā

Footnote [6]:
Just as the glances of Lord Kṛṣṇa shower the water in the form of Spiritual Knowledge on his earnest devotees—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: तपःकृशा देवमीढा आसीद् वर्षीयसी मही । यथैव काम्यतपसः तनुः सम्प्राप्य तत्फलम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tapaḥkṛśā devamīḍhā āsīd varṣīyasī mahī yathaiva kāmyatapasaḥ tanuḥ samprāpya tatphalam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: [7] Just as the shrivelled and emaciated body of a person undergoing severe austerities for the attainment of some desired object, regains its normal health and plumpness after the realization of that object, the earth that was parched and shrivelled through the heat of the sun (in the summer), now swelled up (and became green) when drenched with water by the rain-god.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
This verse shows the pravṛtti mārga of worldly prosperity—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: निशामुखेषु खद्योताः तमसा भान्ति न ग्रहाः । यथा पापेन पाखण्डा न हि वेदाः कलौ युगे ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: niśāmukheṣu khadyotāḥ tamasā bhānti na grahāḥ yathā pāpena pākhaṇḍā na hi vedāḥ kalau yuge
translation: At the nightfall (when the sky is overcast with clouds) it is the fire-flies and not the planets that shine through darkness even as the heretical scriptures dazzle in their sins, in the Kali age and not the Vedas.

devanagari: श्रुत्वा पर्जन्यनिनदं मण्डुकाः व्यसृजन् गिरः । तूष्णीं शयानाः प्राग् यद्वद् ब्राह्मणा नियमात्यये ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvā parjanyaninadaṃ maṇḍukāḥ vyasṛjan giraḥ tūṣṇīṃ śayānāḥ prāg yadvad brāhmaṇā niyamātyaye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śī class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ śī class 2 verb]

translation: Just as young Brāhmaṇa pupils break their silence at the end of their daily religious routine, and begin their recitation of the Vedas on hearing the voice of their preceptor, the hibernating frogs which were lying silent, broke out croaking on hearing the sound of rains (or the thunder of the rainy clouds).

devanagari: आसन् उत्पथवाहिन्यः क्षुद्रनद्योऽनुशुष्यतीः । पुंसो यथास्वतंत्रस्य देहद्रविण सम्पदः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: āsan utpathavāhinyaḥ kṣudranadyo puṃso yathāsvataṃtrasya dehadraviṇa sampadaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [locative single from √ śuṣ class 4 verb]

translation: Rivulets that dry up in the summer, now overflow their banks and flowed into the wrong channel, like the body and riches of persons with unrestrained mind (and enslaved by their senses) and misdirected to the wrong track.

devanagari: हरिता हरिभिः शष्पैः इन्द्रगोपैश्च लोहिता । उच्छिलीन्ध्रकृतच्छाया नृणां श्रीरिव भूरभूत् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: haritā haribhiḥ śaṣpaiḥ indragopaiśca lohitā ucchilīndhrakṛtacchāyā nṛṇāṃ śrīriva bhūrabhūt

translation: Like the army (camp) of a king, the earth appeared green with verdant grass (at some places), crimson-coloured by (the crowds of) Indragopa insects, and at some places, it became covered with the growth of umbrella-shaped mushrooms.

devanagari: क्षेत्राणि शष्यसम्पद्भिः कर्षकाणां मुदं ददुः । मानिनां उनुतापं वै दैवाधीनं अजानताम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣetrāṇi śaṣyasampadbhiḥ karṣakāṇāṃ mudaṃ daduḥ mānināṃ unutāpaṃ vai daivādhīnaṃ ajānatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: [8] Fields with their rich crop brought delight to the farmers and heart-burning[9] to the rich (feeling jealousy towards the farmers) as they did not know that everything depends on the luck.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
SR & KD.: Fields caused joy or grief to the farmers according to the richness or failure of harvests as they did not know that everything depends on destiny.
VT. takes kṣetra as ‘a body’.
Bhāgavata Candrikā: The verse teaches that one should feel joy at the prosperity of others.
Padaratnāvalī interprets kṣetra as ‘a sacred place’ and explains: Due to the vision of the great glory of the Lord, holy places bring joy. But to those who are proud and materialistic and think about the cost of pilgrimage find that the same holy places are full of misery and anxiety. One who is keen on getting Mokṣa (Liberation) should devote himself to the lotus feet of Hari.’

Footnote [9]:
Subodhinī: To the proud kings who are bent on conquering the enemies, the rains and fields covered with crops, cause obstruction and troubles, as they do not know that victory or defeat depends on the Will of the Lord.

devanagari: जलस्थलौकसः सर्वे नववारिनिषेवया । अबिभ्रन् रुचिरं रूपं यथा हरिनिषेवया ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: jalasthalaukasaḥ sarve navavāriniṣevayā abibhran ruciraṃ rūpaṃ yathā hariniṣevayā
translation: [10] Being drenched with the fresh rain-waters,[11] all beings living on the land and in water assumed a beautiful, winsome appearance immediately, even as persons attain to attractive divine form[12] through the service (worship and contemplation) of Hari (due to its being the highest form of religion and of supremely blissful nature).
purport:
Footnote [10]:
VT. endorses Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and states that both the process and attainment (sādhanāvasthā and sādhyāvasthā) of the service of Hari are supremely blissful.
Bhāgavata Candrikā: No Grace can be attained except through the service of Hari.

Footnote [11]:
Padaratnāvalī interprets vāri as ‘knowledge’ that removes the misery of saṃsāra of the denizens of the nether-world (pātāla), inhabitants of the terrestrial globe and residents of the higher (celestial) world. To these inhabitants, pure spiritual knowledge leading to gracious divine forms, is attainable through the service of Hari.

Footnote [12]:
Subodhinī: Just as devotees assume the four-armed divinely refulgent Hari-like form etc. ‘The inhabitants of land etc.’ stand for persons characterised by sattva, rajas and tamas.

devanagari: सरिद्भी सङ्गतः सिन्धुः चुक्षोभ श्वसनोर्मिमान् । अपक्वयोगिनश्चित्तं कामाक्तं गुणयुग् यथा ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: saridbhī saṅgataḥ sindhuḥ cukṣobha śvasanormimān apakvayoginaścittaṃ kāmāktaṃ guṇayug yathā

translation: The sea, the waters of which are already tossed into high waves by stormy winds, becomes more violently agitated at the confluences with rivers, even as the heart of an immature yogī, still influenced with passions, becomes highly perturbed by contacts with worldly objects.[13]
purport:
Footnote [13]:
guṇa—(i) rajo-guṇa—Padaratnāvalī
(ii) Natural propensities creating agitation in the mind for sensual pleasure. These are to be controlled by meditating on Hari.—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: गिरयो वर्षधाराभिः हन्यमाना न विव्यथुः । अभिभूयमाना व्यसनैः यथा अधोक्षजचेतसः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: girayo varṣadhārābhiḥ hanyamānā na vivyathuḥ abhibhūyamānā vyasanaiḥ yathā adhokṣajacetasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Though continuously subjected to the down-pour of rains, the mountains were not agitated at all, like the devotees of Hari whose minds are absorbed in the meditation of the Lord and are not perturbed even under overwhelming calamities (of threefold nature).

devanagari: मार्गा बभूवुः सन्दिग्धाः तृणैश्छन्ना ह्यसंस्कृताः । नाभ्यस्यमानाः श्रुतयो द्विजैः कालहता इव ॥ १६ ॥

translation: Being unused (or unrepaired for a long time) and hence overgrown and covered with grass, the tracks and paths become doubtful (unrecognizable) like the Vedas which are not properly studied (and repeatedly recited), get consigned to oblivion by the force (passage) of time.

devanagari: लोकबन्धुषु मेघेषु विद्युतश्चलसौहृदाः । स्थैर्यं न चक्रुः कामिन्यः पुरुषेषु गुणिष्विव ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: lokabandhuṣu megheṣu vidyutaścalasauhṛdāḥ sthairyaṃ na cakruḥ kāminyaḥ puruṣeṣu guṇiṣviva
translation: Just as pleasure-seeking women bear no constancy to men of virtue and merit (if devoid of money), the fickle-hearted flashes of lightning did not remain constant (and faithful) to clouds which are the friends of the world,

devanagari: धनुर्वियति माहेन्द्रं निर्गुणं च गुणिन्यभात् । व्यक्ते गुणव्यतिकरे अगुणवान् पुरुषो यथा ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanurviyati māhendraṃ nirguṇaṃ ca guṇinyabhāt vyakte guṇavyatikare aguṇavān puruṣo yathā
translation: Just as the attributeless Supreme Person manifests himself in the world which is a product or conglomeration of guṇas, (viz. sattva, rajas and tamas), the rain-bow (Indra’s bow) though devoid of a guṇa (a string). shone forth in the sky, the special quality (guṇa) of which is sound.

devanagari: न रराजोडुपश्छन्नः स्वज्योत्स्नाराजितैर्घनैः । अहंमत्या भासितया स्वभासा पुरुषो यथा ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: na rarājoḍupaśchannaḥ svajyotsnārājitairghanaiḥ ahaṃmatyā bhāsitayā svabhāsā puruṣo yathā

translation: Veiled over by clouds shining with her own beams of light, the moon did not shine forth like the individual soul, enveloped and shrouded in its ego-sense, illumined by its own light.

[nominative plural from √ śram class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śram class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ śram class 4 verb]

translation: [14] Just as persons, at first grown emaciated and exhausted through asceticism, become stout and robust on attaining the fruition of their desires, the trees shrunk and dried up through the heat of the summer, sucked water through their feet (i.e. roots) and assumed many new (rich) forms (putting forth luxuriantly new branches, foliage, fresh blossoms, fruits etc.).
purport:
Footnote [14]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā Persons performing penance with some material end in view grow emaciated through penance, but after realizing the fruition of their desire, become multiplied in the form of sons, grandsons etc., and persons who, with no ulterior motive except the propitiation of the Lord, perforin penance, attain Liberation, and are endowed with various supernatural forms and life similar to the Lord.
Padaratnāvalī interprets pādapa as ‘ascetics’ and explains: As ascetics performing penance, drink the ambrosia of Śāstric knowledge and controlling their senses and minds realize the Supreme Soul (paramātman) Himself.

devanagari: सरःस्वशान्तरोधःसु न्यूषुरङ्गापि सारसाः । गृहेष्वशान्तकृत्येषु ग्राम्या इव दुराशयाः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: saraḥsvaśāntarodhaḥsu nyūṣuraṅgāpi sārasāḥ gṛheṣvaśāntakṛtyeṣu grāmyā iva durāśayāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [locative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [locative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[locative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [locative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [locative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation:
(This verse shows the characteristics of persons permanently indulging in saṃsāra—nitya-saṃsārī).
The cranes continue to stay even in sliding, miry and thorny banks of lakes, Oh Parīkṣit, like unto wicked-minded persons given to low pleasures, continue to live in houses wherein they carry on their detestable cruel (lit. of non-pacific nature) jobs.

devanagari: जलौघैर्निरभिद्यन्त सेतवो वर्षतीश्वरे । पाषण्डिनामसद्वादैः वेदमार्गाः कलौ यथा ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: jalaughairnirabhidyanta setavo varṣatīśvare pāṣaṇḍināmasadvādaiḥ vedamārgāḥ kalau yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vṛṣ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vṛṣ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vṛṣ class 1 verb]

translation: While the god of rains poured down showers of rains, dams and bridges got damaged and breached by floods, just as the paths[15] (of religious duties prescribed for different classes of society (varṇas) and stages of life (āśramas) in the Vedas, are breached by attacks of false heretical theories in the Kali Age.
purport:
Footnote [15]:
Due to the heretical arguments advanced by the Buddha and his followers the Vedic paths of action (Karma), of Knowledge and of Devotion are broken down—Subodhinī Bhakti, however, has a place in non-vedic religions in India.
KD. includes the followers of āgamas and tantras under heretics.

devanagari: व्यमुञ्चन् वान्वायुभिर्नुन्ना भूतेभ्योऽथामृतं घनाः । यथाऽशिषो विश्पतयः काले काले द्विजेरिताः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: vyamuñcan vānvāyubhirnunnā bhūtebhyo yathā

translation: Clouds impelled by winds showered their nectar-like waters to creatures below, just as rulers of the earth or merchant kings advised by Brāhmaṇas at opportune times, distribute their blessed wealth among the subjects (or carry out works of social welfare).

devanagari: एवं वनं तद् वर्षिष्ठं पक्वखर्जुर जम्बुमत् । गोगोपालैर्वृतो रन्तुं सबलः प्राविशद् हरिः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vanaṃ tad varṣiṣṭhaṃ pakvakharjura jambumat gogopālairvṛto rantuṃ sabalaḥ prāviśad hariḥ

[vocative single from √ pac class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pac class 4 verb]

[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: When the forest was thus abounding in trees richly laden with ripe dates and rose apples, Kṛṣṇa, surrounded by cows and cowherds, and accompanied by Balarāma, entered in it for sporting.

devanagari: धेनवो मन्दगामिन्य ऊधोभारेण भूयसा । ययुर्भगवताऽहूता द्रुतं प्रीत्या स्नुतस्तनीः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: dhenavo mandagāminya ūdhobhāreṇa bhūyasā yayurbhagavatā
translation: At the call of Kṛṣṇa, cows which (normally) proceeded in slow gait owing to the weight of heavy udders, hastened with quick steps, showering with joy the milk from their udders on the way.

devanagari: वनौकसः प्रमुदिता वनराजीर्मधुच्युतः । जलधारा गिरेर्नादाद् आसन्ना ददृशे गुहाः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: vanaukasaḥ pramuditā vanarājīrmadhucyutaḥ jaladhārā girernādād āsannā dadṛśe guhāḥ
translation: On the way, Lord Kṛṣṇa saw the inhabitants of the forest (men, birds and beasts) exulted with joy, rows of trees dripping with honey, streams of waters cascading from hills, and their echoing sound in the hills and the caves that were at hand.

devanagari: क्वचिद् वनस्पतिक्रोडे गुहायां चाभिवर्षति । निर्विश्य भगवान् रेमे कन्दमूलफलाशनः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacid vanaspatikroḍe guhāyāṃ cābhivarṣati nirviśya bhagavān reme kandamūlaphalāśanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Sometimes when it rained, the Lord took shelter under a tree or entered a cave, and sported Himself joyously eating bulbs, roots and fruits.

devanagari: दध्योदनं समानीतं शिलायां सलिलान्तिके । सम्भोजनीयैर्बुभुजे गोपैः सङ्कर्षणान्वितः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: dadhyodanaṃ samānītaṃ śilāyāṃ salilāntike sambhojanīyairbubhuje gopaiḥ saṅkarṣaṇānvitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Accompanied by Balarāma, he sat on a slab of stone near water, and ate the rice mixed with curds (brought from home), sharing it with cowherds who were to sit with him for the meal.[16]
purport:
Footnote [16]:
Some other cowherds made their own arrangement at different places—Subodhinī

devanagari: शाद्वलोपरि संविश्य चर्वतो मीलितेक्षणान् । तृप्तान् वृषा वत्सतरान् गाश्च स्वोधोभरश्रमाः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: śādvalopari saṃviśya carvato mīlitekṣaṇān tṛptān vṛṣā vatsatarān gāśca svodhobharaśramāḥ

translation: Beholding the bulls and calves lying on the lush green grass ruminating in great satisfaction, with their eyes closed, and cows fatigued with the heavy weight of their udders (also resting and chewing the cud in satisfaction), and the splendid beauty of the rainy season enhanced by his own enchanting powers, bringing delight to all creatures, the Lord felt gratified (with them all).

devanagari: प्रावृट्श्रियं च तां वीक्ष्य सर्वभूतमुदावहाम् । भगवान् पूजयांचक्रे आत्मशक्त्युपबृंहिताम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: prāvṛṭśriyaṃ ca tāṃ vīkṣya sarvabhūtamudāvahām bhagavān pūjayāṃcakre ātmaśaktyupabṛṃhitām
translation: Beholding the bulls and calves lying on the lush green grass ruminating in great satisfaction, with their eyes closed, and cows fatigued with the heavy weight of their udders (also resting and chewing the cud in satisfaction), and the splendid beauty of the rainy season enhanced by his own enchanting powers, bringing delight to all creatures, the Lord felt gratified (with them all).

devanagari: एवं निवसतोस्तस्मिन् रामकेशवयोर्व्रजे । शरत् समभवद् व्यभ्रा स्वच्छाम्ब्वपरुषानिला ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ nivasatostasmin rāmakeśavayorvraje śarat samabhavad vyabhrā svacchāmbvaparuṣānilā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive dual from √ vas class 1 verb], [locative dual from √ vas class 1 verb]
[genitive dual from √ vas class 1 verb], [locative dual from √ vas class 1 verb]

translation: While Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa were dwelling at that Vraja in this way, the autumn characterised by cloudless skies crystal-like waters and gentle breezes, set in.

devanagari: शरदा नीरजोत्पत्त्या नीराणि प्रकृतिं ययुः । भ्रष्टानामिव चेतांसि पुनर्योगनिषेवया ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: śaradā nīrajotpattyā nīrāṇi prakṛtiṃ yayuḥ bhraṣṭānāmiva cetāṃsi punaryoganiṣevayā
translation: (Originally waters are crystal clear, but they become turbid due to rainy season). Now due to the autumnal season which makes the lotus-plants blossom forth, waters have regained their original translucent nature, just as the minds of persons who have fallen from the path of yoga, regain their mental serenity by resuming their yogic practices.

devanagari: व्योम्नोऽब्भ्रं भूतशाबल्यं भुवः पङ्कमपां मलम् । शरत् जहाराश्रमिणां कृष्णे भक्तिर्यथाशुभम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: vyomno śarat jahārāśramiṇāṃ kṛṣṇe bhaktiryathāśubham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active first single], [aorist active first single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Just as devotion to Kṛṣṇa removes all the evils setting upon persons belonging to different stages of life (āśramas)[17], the Autumn dispelled all the clouds from the sky and prevented the contacts of the elements or the congestion of created beings, dried up the mire on the earth, and cleansed the turbidity from water.
purport:
Footnote [17]:
SR. enumerates the evils: The trouble of physical work during studenthood, the pangs of separation in married state, and sexual passions etc in sannyāsins.

devanagari: सर्वस्वं जलदा हित्वा विरेजुः शुभ्रवर्चसः । यथा त्यक्तैषणाः शान्ता मुनयो मुक्तकिल्बिषाः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvasvaṃ jaladā hitvā virejuḥ śubhravarcasaḥ yathā tyaktaiṣaṇāḥ śāntā munayo muktakilbiṣāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]

[nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: Having discharged (and thus renounced) all their possessions (in the form of the waters in them), the clouds shone forth in their spotless white splendour, as quiescent sages who have absolved themselves of all sins do, after renouncing all their three-fold desires (about progeny, property and celebrity).

devanagari: गिरयो मुमुचुस्तोयं क्वचिन्न मुमुचुः शिवम् । यथा ज्ञानामृतं काले ज्ञानिनो ददते न वा ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: girayo mumucustoyaṃ kvacinna mumucuḥ śivam yathā jñānāmṛtaṃ kāle jñānino dadate na vā
translation: Just as people with spiritual wisdom impart their nectar-like knowledge on some occasions, and refuse to do so on others, the mountains also at some places allowed the streams of pure water to flow, while at others they withheld them.

devanagari: नैवाविदन् क्षीयमाणं जलं गाधजलेचराः । यथाऽयुरन्वहं क्षय्यं नरा मूढाः कुटुम्बिनः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: naivāvidan kṣīyamāṇaṃ jalaṃ gādhajalecarāḥ yathā

[nominative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ muh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ muh class 4 verb]

translation: Just as ignorant persons immersed in their household matters are not cognisant of the daily shortening of the span of their life, so the creatures in shallow waters did not perceive the diminishing water level every day.

devanagari: गाधवारिचरास्तापं अविन्दन् शरदर्कजम् । यथा दरिद्रः कृपणः कुटुम्ब्यविजितेन्द्रियः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: gādhavāricarāstāpaṃ avindan śaradarkajam yathā daridraḥ kṛpaṇaḥ kuṭumbyavijitendriyaḥ
translation: Just as a poverty-stricken, wretched man burdened with family responsibilities but of uncontrolled senses, undergoes miseries, the creatures in shallow waters suffered from the heat of the autumnal sun.

devanagari: शनैः शनैर्जहुः पङ्कं स्थलान्यामं च वीरुधः । यथाहंममतां धीराः शरीरादिष्वनात्मसु ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: śanaiḥ śanairjahuḥ paṅkaṃ sthalānyāmaṃ ca vīrudhaḥ yathāhaṃmamatāṃ dhīrāḥ śarīrādiṣvanātmasu
translation: Marshy tracts of land gradually gave up their marshiness, and the plants their unripeness, just as (spiritually) wise men slowly (step by step) discard their notions of ‘I’ and ‘mine’ with respect to their bodies and belongings which are distinctly different from their self.

devanagari: निश्चलाम्बुरभूत् तूष्णीं समुद्रः शरदागमे । आत्मनि उपरते सम्यङ् मुनिर्व्युपरतागमः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: niścalāmburabhūt tūṣṇīṃ samudraḥ śaradāgame ātmani uparate samyaṅ munirvyuparatāgamaḥ
translation: When the season of autumn set in, the waters of the ocean grew calm and still, even as a meditative sage established in his self, gives up ritualistic performances and recitation of the Vedas.

devanagari: केदारेभ्यस्त्वपोऽगृह्णन् कर्षका दृढसेतुभिः । यथा प्राणैः स्रवज्ज्ञानं तन्निरोधेन योगिनः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: kedārebhyastvapo yathā prāṇaiḥ sravajjñānaṃ tannirodhena yoginaḥ
translation: Cultivators secured the supply of water to their fields by strengthening the bunds (and thus stopping the breaches and leakages), just as Yogins, by control of senses, preserve their spiritual knowledge which otherwise gets dissipated (through unsubdued senses)[18].
purport:
Footnote [18]:
Subodhinī aptly quotes:
ūrdhvendriyais tu vikṣepo jñānasyā'dho vināśanam /
nirodhe puñja-bhāvena svakāryaṃ sādhayed dhruvam //

devanagari: शरदर्कांशुजांस्तापान् भूतानां उडुपोऽहरत् । देहाभिमानजं बोधो मुकुन्दो व्रजयोषिताम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: śaradarkāṃśujāṃstāpān bhūtānāṃ uḍupo dehābhimānajaṃ bodho mukundo vrajayoṣitām
translation: The moon relieved the sufferings of creatures caused by the rays (heat) of the autumnal sun, just as the (revelation of) spiritual knowledge alleviates the troubles caused by the (false) identification of the body with the soul, or Lord Kṛṣṇa removes the pangs of separation of (cowherd) women of Vraja.

devanagari: खमशोभत निर्मेघं शरद् विमलतारकम् । सत्त्वयुक्तं यथा चित्तं शब्दब्रह्मार्थदर्शनम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: khamaśobhata nirmeghaṃ śarad vimalatārakam sattvayuktaṃ yathā cittaṃ śabdabrahmārthadarśanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: The cloudless sky shone forth beautifully with bright stars twinkling clearly in autumnal nights, as the mind with the predominance of Sattva, correctly comprehends the import of the Vedas (Śabda-Brahman) as decided by the Pūrva and Uttara Mīmāṃsās.

devanagari: अखण्डमण्डलो व्योम्नि रराजोडुगणैः शशी । यथा यदुपतिः कृष्णो वृष्णिचक्रावृतो भुवि ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: akhaṇḍamaṇḍalo vyomni rarājoḍugaṇaiḥ śaśī yathā yadupatiḥ kṛṣṇo vṛṣṇicakrāvṛto bhuvi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The full moon, surrounded by stars, shone resplendent in the heavens, just as Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of Yadus, did in his circle of Vṛṣṇi clansmen, on the earth.

devanagari: आश्लिष्य समशीतोष्णं प्रसून वनमारुतम् । जनास्तापं जहुर्गोप्यो न कृष्णहृतचेतसः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: āśliṣya samaśītoṣṇaṃ prasūna vanamārutam janāstāpaṃ jahurgopyo na kṛṣṇahṛtacetasaḥ
translation: People, embraced by the (cooling) temperate breeze which has blown over blossoming forests, felt relieved of the heat (of the sun) but not so the Gopa women whose heart was stolen (captivated) by Kṛṣṇa (whom they embraced in their hearts) as the pangs of actual separation were enhanced thereby.

devanagari: गावो मृगाः खगा नार्यः पुष्पिण्यः शरदाभवन् । अन्वीयमानाः स्ववृषैः फलैरीशक्रिया इव ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: gāvo mṛgāḥ khagā nāryaḥ puṣpiṇyaḥ śaradābhavan anvīyamānāḥ svavṛṣaiḥ phalairīśakriyā iva
translation: Due to the (fecundating influence of the) Autumn, and through the insistent courting of their mates, cows, female deer (or beasts), she-birds and women, conceived (irrespective of their willingness), just as the acts for propitiating the Lord are followed by the concomitant fruit (automatically).

devanagari: उदहृष्यन् वारिजानि सूर्योत्थाने कुमुद् विना । राज्ञा तु निर्भया लोका यथा दस्यून् विना नृप ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: udahṛṣyan vārijāni sūryotthāne kumud vinā rājñā tu nirbhayā lokā yathā dasyūn vinā nṛpa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: At the sunrise all lotuses (except moon-lotuses) bloomed forth, just as all subjects except criminals (or robbers) become fearless on the installation of a king (i.e. restoration of good government).

devanagari: पुरग्रामेष्वाग्रयणैः इन्द्रियैश्च महोत्सवैः । बभौ भूः पक्वसस्याढ्या कलाभ्यां नितरां हरेः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: puragrāmeṣvāgrayaṇaiḥ indriyaiśca mahotsavaiḥ babhau bhūḥ pakvasasyāḍhyā kalābhyāṃ nitarāṃ hareḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[vocative single from √ pac class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pac class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ pac class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pac class 4 verb]

translation: The earth, rich with ripe cornfields, shone bright and beautiful, especially due to the incarnation of the two rays (aṃśas) of Lord Hari (viz. Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) and also with the performance of Vedic sacrifices of harvest season (wherein new com is offered), and with festivities and grand celebrations gratifying the senses, which were carried on in cities and villages.

devanagari: वणिङ्मुनि नृपस्नाता निर्गम्यार्थान् प्रपेदिरे । वर्षरुद्धा यथा सिद्धाः स्वपिण्डान् काल आगते ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: vaṇiṅmuni nṛpasnātā nirgamyārthān prapedire varṣaruddhā yathā siddhāḥ svapiṇḍān kāla āgate

[nominative plural from √ rudh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ rudh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ rudh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ rudh class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ rudh class 7 verb]
[nominative plural from √ rudh class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ rudh class 7 verb], [accusative plural from √ rudh class 7 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb]

translation:
Just as Siddhas (the masters of yoga and mantras—mystic incantations), detained (in their present bodies) by their ordained span of life, attain their respective desired heavenly bodies, when the appropriate time arrives, merchants, sages, kings and snātakas (life-long bachelor-students) shut up in their places due to the rainy-season, (and were impeded from undertaking an expedition or journey) now set out to achieve their respective objects.
